name: Ma'ili Pillbox (Pink Pillbox)
tagline: The West Side's colorful pillbox hike with sunset views that absolutely crush the East Side versions.
parking: Free parking at the base trailhead on Ma'ili Point — small gravel lot
bestTime: Late afternoon for the best sunset — West Side faces the ocean sunset directly
talkStory: These WWII bunkers were part of the Waianae coast defense network, built after Pearl Harbor to watch for Japanese landing forces along every ridge with ocean sightlines. Local artists transform the pillboxes with colorful murals that change regularly — the 'Pink Pillbox' nickname comes from the ever-evolving graffiti art. The leeward location means this hike is almost always dry and sunny. The sun drops directly into the Pacific — locals say it's the best unobstructed sunset on the island.
